title: Acoustic transducer element
abstract: In an embodiment, an acoustic transducer includes an element with an acoustic radiative surface having two warped edges at opposing sides. In another embodiment, an acoustic transducer includes first and second elements, each divided into at least two spatially separated portions electrically coupled to each other, the portions configured to interleave. In a further embodiment, an acoustic transducer includes first and second transducer elements. The second element is situated adjacent to the first element and includes a radiative surface with an edge having periodic elongations. In yet another embodiment, an acoustic transducer includes a transducer element with an acoustic radiative surface that has a skewed diamond shape.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An acoustic transducer comprising: a transducer element with an acoustic radiative surface, the radiative surface including two warped edges at opposing sides of the radiative surface. 2. The transducer of Claim 1, wherein the radiative surface further includes two tapered ends, and wherein each warped edge extends from one of the tapered ends to the other. 3. The transducer of Claim 1, wherein each of the two warped edges has a truncated Gaussian shape. 4. The transducer of Claim 1, wherein the transducer element is a first transducer element, the transducer further comprising a second transducer element, with an acoustic radiative surface, the radiative surface including two warped edges at opposing sides of the radiative surface. 5. The transducer of Claim 4, wherein the first and second transducer elements are stacked against each other, the acoustic radiative surface of the first transducer element and the acoustic radiative surface of the second transducer element partially overlapping. 6. The transducer of Claim 4, further comprising an electrical circuit configured to drive or receive signals by the first and second transducer elements at the same frequency, the electrical circuit further configured to acquire interferometric sonar data based on signals received by the transducer elements. 7. The transducer of Claim 4, wherein each of the first and second transducer elements is divided into at least two spatially separated element portions electrically coupled to each other, the element portions of the first transducer configured to interleave the element portions of the second transducer. 8. The transducer of Claim 4, wherein the first and second transducer elements occupy a common surface and overlap each other. 9. The transducer of Claim 8, wherein the first transducer element comprises a first plurality of non-contiguous sub-elements electrically coupled to each other, and wherein the second transducer element comprises a second plurality of noncontiguous sub-elements electrically coupled to each other, and wherein the first plurality of sub-elements is electrically isolated from the second plurality of sub- elements. 10. The transducer of Claim 1, wherein the transducer element is a PVDF transducer element. 11. The transducer of Claim 1, wherein the transducer element is a ceramic, capacitive, fiber-optic, magnetostrictive, or electrostrictive transducer element. 12. The transducer of Claim 1, further including one or more additional transducer elements, each element having a respective acoustic radiative surface including two warped edges at opposing sides of the respective radiative surface. 13. The transducer of Claim 1, wherein each of the two warped edges has a periodic shape. 14. The transducer of Claim 1, wherein each of the two warped edges is smooth. 15. The transducer of Claim 1, wherein each of the two warped edges defines at least one 90Â° angle therein, the two warped edges further defining a second portion of the transducer element shifted from a first portion of the transducer element in a direction perpendicular to a major axis of the transducer element. 16. An acoustic transducer comprising: a first transducer element divided into at least two spatially separated element portions electrically coupled to each other; and a second transducer element divided into at least two spatially separated element portions electrically coupled to each other, the element portions of the second transducer configured to interleave the element portions of the first transducer. 17. The transducer of Claim 16, further comprising an electrical drive circuit configured to drive or receive signals by the first and second transducer elements at the same frequency. 18. The transducer of Claim 17, wherein the electrical drive circuit is further configured to drive the first and second transducer elements with mutually distinct phases. 19. The transducer of Claim 16, further comprising an electrical receive circuit configured to detect a phase difference between acoustic signals intended to be received at the first and second transducer elements. 20. The transducer of Claim 16, wherein the element portions of the first and second transducer elements are rectangular in shape and have longer sides oriented parallel to one another and to axes along which the two transducer elements, respectively, are divided. 21. The transducer of Claim 16, wherein at least one element portion of the second transducer element is in physical contact with at least one element portion of the first transducer element. 22. The transducer of Claim 16, wherein the first and second transducer elements are PVDF, ceramic, capacitive, fiber-optic, magnetostrictive, or electrostrictive transducer element. 23. The transducer of Claim 16, wherein each of the spatially separated element portions of the first and second transducer elements has an acoustic radiative surface with two warped edges at opposing sides of the radiative surface. 24. The transducer of Claim 17, wherein each of the warped edges has a truncated Gaussian shape. 25. The transducer of Claim 16, further including one or more additional transducer elements, each additional element divided into at least two spatially separated element portions electrically coupled to each other. 26. An acoustic transducer comprising: a transducer element having an acoustic radiative surface with an edge having periodic elongations protruding therefrom. 27. The transducer of Claim 26, wherein the edge with periodic elongations has a sawtooth or sinusoidal shape. 28. The transducer of Claim 26, wherein the transducer element is a first transducer element, the acoustic transducer further including a second transducer element having an edge with periodic elongations protruding therefrom, the first and second transducer elements situated adjacent to one another. 29. The transducer of Claim 28, wherein the periodic elongations of the second transducer element are offset from the periodic elongations of the first transducer element, and wherein the edges with periodic elongations of the first and second elements fit against each other to form a common edge. 30. The transducer of Claim 28, further comprising an electrical drive circuit configured to drive or receive signals by the first and second transducer elements at the same frequency. 31. The transducer of Claim 30, wherein the electrical circuit is further configured to drive the first and second transducer elements with mutually distinct phases. 32. The transducer of Claim 28, wherein the edge having periodic elongations protruding therefrom is a warped edge, the transducer element further including an additional warped edge, the warped edge and the additional warped edge at opposing sides of the radiative surface of the transducer element. 33. The transducer of Claim 32, wherein the additional warped edge has a truncated Gaussian shape. 34. The transducer of Claim 28, wherein the second transducer element is stacked against the first transducer element, the radiative surface of the second transducer element overlapping with a radiative surface of the first transducer element. 35. The transducer of Claim 26, wherein the transducer element is a PVDF transducer element. 36. The transducer of Claim 26, wherein the transducer element is a ceramic, capacitive, fiber-optic, magnetostrictive, or electrostrictive transducer element. 37. An acoustic transducer comprising: 

 a first transducer element divided into first and second spatially separated element portions electrically coupled to each other; and a second transducer element divided into first and second spatially separated element portions electrically coupled to each other, An acoustic transducer comprising: a transducer element with an acoustic radiative surface that has a skewed diamond shape. The acoustic transducer of Claim 38, wherein the transducer element is a first transducer element, the acoustic transducer further comprising a second transducer element with an acoustic radiative surface that has a skewed diamond shape. The acoustic transducer of Claim 39, wherein the first and second transducer elements form part of an interferometric transducer array with a long axis and a short axis, each of the first and second transducer elements having a respective acoustic center and a width along the short axis, a center-to-center spacing along the short axis between the respective acoustic centers being smaller than the width or the first or second transducer element. The acoustic transducer of Claim 38, wherein the transducer element is a PVDF transducer element.
